info.title: Koyeb Rest API
info.description: The Koyeb API allows you to interact with the Koyeb platform in a simple, programmatic way using conventional HTTP requests.

parameters.name: types
parameters.description: (Optional) Filter on object type

401.description: Returned when the token is not valid.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to access the resource.

404.description: Returned when the resource does not exist.

500.description: Returned in case of server error.

get.description: This endpoint opens a websocket. Once open, all frames going through the websocket should be formatted in JSON. Input frames should match the format specified below. Output frames will match the response schema.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the resource to exec on.

parameters.name: body.command
parameters.description: Command to exec. Mandatory in the first frame sent

parameters.name: body.stdin.data
parameters.description: Data is base64 encoded

parameters.name: body.stdin.close
parameters.description: Indicate last data frame

parameters.name: body.disableTty
parameters.description: Disable TTY. It's enough to specify it in the first frame

parameters.name: id_type
parameters.description: When specified, it is used to determine if the kind of resource the id refers to. If missing, defaults to the instance id.

401.description: Returned when the token is not valid.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to access the resource.

404.description: Returned when the resource does not exist.

500.description: Returned in case of server error.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of logs to retrieve, either "build" or "runtime". Defaults to "runtime".

parameters.name: app_id
parameters.description: (Optional) Filter on the provided app_id. At least one of app_id, service_id, deployment_id, regional_deployment_id or instance_ids must be set.

parameters.name: service_id
parameters.description: (Optional) Filter on the provided service_id. At least one of app_id, service_id, deployment_id, regional_deployment_id or instance_ids must be set.

parameters.name: deployment_id
parameters.description: (Optional) Filter on the provided deployment_id. At least one of app_id, service_id, deployment_id, regional_deployment_id or instance_ids must be set.

parameters.name: regional_deployment_id
parameters.description: (Optional) Filter on the provided regional_deployment_id. At least one of app_id, service_id, deployment_id, regional_deployment_id or instance_ids must be set.

parameters.name: instance_id
parameters.description: Deprecated, prefer using instance_ids instead.

parameters.name: instance_ids
parameters.description: (Optional) Filter on the provided instance_ids. At least one of app_id, service_id, deployment_id, regional_deployment_id or instance_ids must be set.

parameters.name: stream
parameters.description: Deprecated, prefer using streams instead.

parameters.name: streams
parameters.description: (Optional) Filter on stream: either "stdout", "stderr" or "koyeb" (for system logs).

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: (Optional) Must always be before `end`. Defaults to 15 minutes ago.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: (Optional) Must always be after `start`. Defaults to now.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: (Optional) `asc` or `desc`. Defaults to `desc`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: (Optional) Defaults to 100. Maximum of 1000.

parameters.name: regex
parameters.description: (Optional) Apply a regex to filter logs. Can't be used with `text`.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: (Optional) Looks for this string in logs. Can't be used with `regex`.

parameters.name: regions
parameters.description: (Optional) Filter on the provided regions (e.g. ["fra", "was"]).

401.description: Returned when the token is not valid.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to access the resource.

404.description: Returned when the resource does not exist.

500.description: Returned in case of server error.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of logs to retrieve, either "build" or "runtime". Defaults to "runtime".

parameters.name: app_id
parameters.description: (Optional) Filter on the provided app_id. At least one of app_id, service_id, deployment_id, regional_deployment_id or instance_ids must be set.

parameters.name: stream
parameters.description: Deprecated, prefer using streams instead.

parameters.name: streams
parameters.description: (Optional) Filter on stream: either "stdout", "stderr" or "koyeb" (for system logs).

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: (Optional) Defaults to 24 hours ago.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: (Optional) Defaults to 1000. Maximum of 1000.

parameters.name: regex
parameters.description: (Optional) Apply a regex to filter logs. Can't be used with `text`.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: (Optional) Looks for this string in logs. Can't be used with `regex`.

parameters.name: regions
parameters.description: (Optional) Filter on the provided regions (e.g. ["fra", "was"]).

parameters.name: service_id
parameters.description: ID of the service to query instances metrics for. Ignored if instance_id is set.

parameters.name: instance_id
parameters.description: ID of the instance to query metrics for.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Metric to query.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: (Optional) Defaults to an hour prior to end.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: (Optional) Defaults to now.

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: (Optional) Must be a valid duration in hours (h) or minutes (m). Defaulst to 5m.

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description:
(Optional) Filter by status

 - SNAPSHOT_STATUS_INVALID: zero value, invalid
 - SNAPSHOT_STATUS_CREATING: the snapshot is being created
 - SNAPSHOT_STATUS_AVAILABLE: the snapshot is complete and available
 - SNAPSHOT_STATUS_MIGRATING: the snapshot is being migrated
 - SNAPSHOT_STATUS_DELETING: the snapshot is being deleted
 - SNAPSHOT_STATUS_DELETED: the snapshot is deleted

post.description:
CreateAccessToken creates a short-lived access token in the scope of the
specified organization, provided the user making the request is part of
said organization.

It's possible to specify a validity for the token, which defaults to 1h
and must be no more than 24h. The format is `<number>s`, where `<number>`
is a floating point in seconds (so `123.456789012s` means 123 seconds and
456789012 nanoseconds). See:
https://protobuf.dev/reference/php/api-docs/Google/Protobuf/Duration.html.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Organization id for ephemeral credential

post.description:
UnscopeOrganizationToken removes the organization scope from a token. This
endpoint is useful when a user wants to remove an organization: by
unscoping the token first, the user can then delete the organization
without invalidating his token.

google.protobuf.NullValue.description:
`NullValue` is a singleton enumeration to represent the null value for the
`Value` type union.

 The JSON representation for `NullValue` is JSON `null`.

 - NULL_VALUE: Null value.

has_next.title: If there is more items after in the collection

success.title: Whether the image is accessible or not

reason.title: (Optional) If the image is not accessible, the reason

code.title: (Optional) If the image is not accessible, return an error code

VerifyDockerImageReply.ErrCode.description:
- UNKNOWN: Default value
 - AUTH_ACCESS_DENIED: The registry denied access to an authenticated request
 - ANON_ACCESS_DENIED: The registry denied access to an anonymous request
 - AUTH_NOT_FOUND: The image has not been found after an authenticated request
 - ANON_NOT_FOUND: The image has not been found after an anonymous request
 - REGISTRY_ERROR: The registry returned an error
 - TIMEOUT: The request to the registry timed out
 - DNS: There was an error trying to resolve the name of the registry
 - MALFORMED: The provided image name is malformed
 - INVALID_OS: The operating system is not supported
 - INVALID_ARCH: The architecture is not supported
 - INVALID_SCHEME: The scheme is not https
 - GENERIC: Generic catch-all error code
VerifyDockerImageReply.ErrCode.title: The error code associated to each specific failure mode

id.title: The ID of the archive to deploy

build_command.title: A command used to override the build command, run after all build steps

run_command.title: A command used to override the default run command

privileged.title: A flag to run the container in privileged mode

path.title: the path where the file is copied

permissions.title: the permissions of the file in format 0644

grace_period.title: An optional initial period in seconds to wait for the instance to become healthy, default is 5s

interval.title: An optional period in seconds between two health checks, default is 60s

restart_limit.title: An optional number of consecutive failures before attempting to restart the service, default is 3

timeout.title: An optional maximum time to wait in seconds before considering the check as a failure, default is 5s

sha.description: The git sha for this build (we resolve the reference at the start of the build).

image.description: The docker image built as a result of this build.

wait_completion.description: This flag is used to finalize the build, and continue the deployment in case of success, or cancel and potentially retry the build in case of failure.

quantile.description:
The quantile to use for autoscaling. For example, set to 95 to use the 95th
percentile (p95) for autoscaling.  Valid values are between 0 and 100.

value.description:
DEPRECATED: use deep_sleep_value instead.
Delay in seconds after which a service which received 0 request is put to deep sleep.

deep_sleep_value.description: Delay in seconds after which a service which received 0 request is put to deep sleep.

light_sleep_value.description: Delay in seconds after which a service which received 0 request is put to light sleep.

DeploymentStrategyType.description:
- DEPLOYMENT_STRATEGY_TYPE_INVALID: DEPRECATED: Use DEPLOYMENT_STRATEGY_TYPE_DEFAULT instead.
 - DEPLOYMENT_STRATEGY_TYPE_DEFAULT: Default/unspecified strategy (resolves to platform default, currently ROLLING).
 - DEPLOYMENT_STRATEGY_TYPE_CANARY: Use canary strategy.
 - DEPLOYMENT_STRATEGY_TYPE_ROLLING: Use rolling strategy.
 - DEPLOYMENT_STRATEGY_TYPE_BLUE_GREEN: Use blue green strategy.
 - DEPLOYMENT_STRATEGY_TYPE_IMMEDIATE: Use immediate strategy.

privileged.title: A flag to run the container in privileged mode
A flag to run the container in privileged mode

repository.description: A url to a git repository (contains the provider as well) .e.g: github.com/koyeb/test.

branch.title: A git branch that will be tracked for new commits and deployments will be created

tag.title: A git tag that should be built

sha.title: A git commit that should be built (useful for pinning to a commit, this will always be set when a deployment is created by a code push)

build_command.title: A command used to override the build command, run after all build steps — deprecated, use buildpack.build_command instead

run_command.title: A command used to override the default run command - deprecated, use buildpack.run_command instead

no_deploy_on_push.title: A flag to disable a new deployment when a push event is detected

workdir.title: A subdirectory to use as the build directory

port.title: The port to use to perform the health check, must be declared in the ports section

path.title: The path to use to perform the HTTP health check

method.title: An optional HTTP method to use to perform the health check, default is GET

headers.title: An optional list of HTTP headers to provide when performing the request, default is empty

has_next.title: If there is more items after in the collection

replica_index.title: the replica index to mount the volume to

port.title: The port to use to perform the health check, must be declared in the ports section

snapshot_id.title: (optional) the id of the snapshot whence the volume comes from

organization_id.title: the organization to which the volume belongs to

service_id.title: the service_id to which the volume is eventually bound to

region.title: the region where the volume exists

read_only.title: whether to mount the volume in read-only mode

max_size.title: the maximum size of the volume (in Gigabyte / GB)

cur_size.title: the used amount of space as measured the last time (in Gigabyte / GB)

PersistentVolume.title: The object that represent a volume to handle persistency for deployments

PersistentVolumeBackingStore.title:
- PERSISTENT_VOLUME_BACKING_STORE_INVALID: zero value, invalid
 - PERSISTENT_VOLUME_BACKING_STORE_LOCAL_BLK: the backing store is a locally reachable block device

PersistentVolumeStatus.title:
- PERSISTENT_VOLUME_STATUS_INVALID: zero value, invalid
 - PERSISTENT_VOLUME_STATUS_ATTACHED: the volume is attached to an instance
 - PERSISTENT_VOLUME_STATUS_DETACHED: the volume is free to use
 - PERSISTENT_VOLUME_STATUS_DELETING: the volume will be deleted
 - PERSISTENT_VOLUME_STATUS_DELETED: the volume was deleted
 - PERSISTENT_VOLUME_STATUS_ARCHIVING: the volume is being archived (snapshot being created and migrated to remote storage)

region.title: the region where the snapshot resides, if any

type.title: the type of snapshot (can be local or remote)

Snapshot.description: The object that represents a snapshot. It can either be local, on a node, or remote, in a cold storage.

SnapshotStatus.title:
- SNAPSHOT_STATUS_INVALID: zero value, invalid
 - SNAPSHOT_STATUS_CREATING: the snapshot is being created
 - SNAPSHOT_STATUS_AVAILABLE: the snapshot is complete and available
 - SNAPSHOT_STATUS_MIGRATING: the snapshot is being migrated
 - SNAPSHOT_STATUS_DELETING: the snapshot is being deleted
 - SNAPSHOT_STATUS_DELETED: the snapshot is deleted

SnapshotType.title:
- SNAPSHOT_TYPE_INVALID: zero value, invalid
 - SNAPSHOT_TYPE_LOCAL: the snapshot is local to the machine
 - SNAPSHOT_TYPE_REMOTE: the snapshot is remote in a cold storage

project_id.description: The project ID this secret belongs to. Empty if the secret is organization-level.

usage.title: The computed usage of instances per month

replica_index.title: optionally, explicitly choose the replica index to mount the volume to

skip_build.description:
If set to true, the build stage will be skipped and the image coming from the last successful build step will be used instead.
The call fails if no previous successful builds happened.

version.title: A version updated whenever this state changes (useful for caching)

skip_build.description:
If set to true, the build stage will be skipped and the image coming from the last successful build step will be used instead.
The call fails if no previous successful builds happened.

save_only.title: If set, do not trigger a deployment, only store the new settings

id.description: The archive id, that can be referenced when creating or updating a service.

upload_url.description:
The URL where to upload the archive. This URL is signed and can only be
used to upload the archive until `valid_until`.

size.description: The provisioned space for the archive.

created_at.description: Date of creation of the archive.

deleted_at.description:
This field is automatically set by Koyeb when the
archive is garbage collected.

size.description: How much space to provision for the archive, in bytes.

project_id.title: (Optional) The project ID to associate with the archive

email.title: The email of the person to invite

max_total_size.description: MaxTotalSize for all volumes on a region (in Gigabyte / GB).

max_volume_size.description: MaxVolumeSize for one volume (in Gigabyte / GB).

max_per_instance_size.description: MaxPerInstanceSize for all volumes on an instance (in Gigabyte / GB).

access_reserved_subdomains.title: The mapping between reserved_subdomain names and their value is in the code

UserFlags.title:
- ADMIN: A user is an admin user
 - TEST: A user is a test user
 - RESTRICTED: Whether this account as restricted access
 - ACTIVE: Whether this account is active
 - BETA: Account in beta program
 - MAX_ORGANIZATIONS_25: User can create this much organizations, overriding the default amount
 - PARTNER_CSP: User can create sub-organizations
 - IGNORE_ORGANIZATION_NAME_RESERVATION_RULE_NEON_PREFIX: User can bypass organization name reservations rules

service_count.title: Number of services in this project

by_status.title: Number of apps grouped by status
Number of domains grouped by status

by_type.title: Number of instances grouped by type

invitations_by_status.title: Number of invitations grouped by status

by_instance_type.title: Number of databases grouped by instance type

services.title: Number of services grouped by type

by_type.title: Number of secrets grouped by type

by_status.title: Number of services grouped by status

GetOAuthOptionsReply.description: A list of providers which you can use for single sign-on.

url.title: The url to start the installation flow

state.title:
The state required by the protocol, it is only valid 10 minutes and encodes
information about the type of flow

metadata.title:
A small (limited to 400 characters) string of arbitrary metadata which will
be encoded in the state

has_next.title: If there is more items after in the collection

token.title: A jwt token to be used for session

state.title: The state created at the origin of the OAuth flow

code.title: The code returned by the OAuth provider

setup_action.description:
setup_action is populated in the context of a GitHub app installation
request. For logins and signups, it is not set.

installation_id.description:
installation_id is populated in the context of a GitHub app installation
request. For logins and signups, it is not set.

id.title: The name of the provider (.e.g github, google)

url.title: The URL to call to initiate the OAuth flow

state.title:
The OAuth state required by the protocol, it is only valid 10 minutes and
encodes information about the type of flow
